name: Philips Hue
short_description: A line of connected smart LED bulbs and accessories controlled by app, voice, and a Zigbee bridge.
long_description: Philips Hue, launched in 2012 as one of the first commercially available smart bulbs, turns ordinary room lighting into an addressable, programmable layer of the home. A central bridge links bulbs over a Zigbee mesh and exposes them to apps, schedules, sensors, and voice assistants like Alexa, Google Assistant, and Siri. As infrastructure-scale, ambient physical AI, it shows how intelligence can disappear into everyday fixtures: responding to presence, routines, and context rather than reading as a distinct device.

images.caption: A Philips Hue A19 smart LED bulb shown lit in colored light.
images.alt_text: A Philips Hue A19 screw-base smart LED bulb glowing in colored light against a neutral background.

sources.title: Introducing Philips hue: the world's smartest LED bulb, marking a new era in home lighting
